title: Monorepo + pnpm + TS/ESM + static-site + CI (build/test/lint) skeleton
context: Know-Your-Rights needs an agent-neutral publishing pipeline before any rights content is written. This skeleton task sets up the monorepo structure — TypeScript, ESM, pnpm workspaces — with a static-site app placeholder and a CI pipeline (build, test, lint) that all subsequent tasks build on. No vendor-specific or agent-specific logic may appear in the core packages; anything tool-specific lives in adapters/. This is a pure engineering/infrastructure task and carries the lowest risk tier.
objective: Bootstrap the Know-Your-Rights monorepo with pnpm workspaces, TypeScript/ESM configuration, a static-site app skeleton (Astro or Eleventy — open question, pick the most accessible/offline-friendly option), and a CI pipeline that runs build, test, and lint. The skeleton must be agent-neutral, pass pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint from the root, and be ready for the content schema (task 003) and content-lint suite (task 004) to be wired in.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm workspaces configured with at least packages/kyr-schema, lib/editorial, apps/site, and scripts/ packages or directories stubbed out
TypeScript (strict mode) + ESM throughout; tsconfig references wired correctly for monorepo builds
Static-site app skeleton (Astro or Eleventy) renders a placeholder index page with no-JS fallback and a print stylesheet stub
CI pipeline (GitHub Actions) runs pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint on push/PR and reports pass/fail
pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint all pass from the repo root with zero errors
No vendor-specific, agent-specific, or API-key logic in any core package; CLAUDE.md agent-neutral-core rule satisfied
No secrets, tokens, or PII in any committed file; dependency scanning stub present in CI
output: A merged PR containing the monorepo skeleton: pnpm workspace configuration, TypeScript/ESM build config, static-site app stub, and a passing CI pipeline (build/test/lint green).
